----- *Important Notice!!

Welcome to Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We ship worldwide!!


Thank you for viewing!


Please feel free to contact us with any questions.


Also, please carefully read the shop policy located below the guitar description.


-----------------------------------------

This is a store-exclusive, fully custom-ordered model built by MOON to specifications chosen by our Ikebukuro store's bass specialist!

It turned out incredibly cool!

But what makes it even more impressive than the specs is its astonishingly light weight!



【About this specific unit】

1. Weight

Despite being a 5-string model with an ash body, it weighs in at just 3.95kg!

A 5-string ash bass weighing under 4kg is something that rarely happens unless you specifically order it and have lightweight wood carefully selected for the build.

You might worry that a lighter weight leads to a thin sound, but MOON delivers as expected.

The moment you play it, you get a bold, punchy sound that feels bright and immediate, and the lowest notes on the 5th string remain clear and defined without any muddiness.

Hearing about such ultra-light specs might make some worry about "head dive," but rest assured.

When actually playing this instrument while seated, simply resting your right arm lightly on the body—as you normally would—makes head dive a non-issue.

You get all the benefits of incredible lightness and superb handling, allowing you to immerse yourself in playing without any stress.



2. Setup

After the instrument arrived, we adjusted the string action to be slightly low: approx. 1.8mm on the 1st string side and 2.5mm on the 5th string side.

This allows for easy fretting and responsive picking with minimal effort, while ensuring smooth playability where your fingers don't get snagged during popping techniques.

*Note: With a lower action setting, players with a heavy picking hand may experience some fret buzz.

If that happens, we can readjust the action to the optimal height for your playing style, so please feel free to let us know.



◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆



【Key Customization Points】

Inspired by 70s styles, we selected ash—known for its distinctive grain—as the body wood.

We ordered it with a beautiful transparent black finish to accentuate that grain. It features a luxurious matching headstock, where the raised gold MOON logo exudes a unique, sophisticated vibe that appeals to seasoned players.



It incorporates 70s-style white binding and square block inlays on the fretboard, while the use of chrome hardware tones down the flashiness, creating an air of mature elegance.

For the finish, we selected a durable urethane coating that withstands harsh environmental changes—a choice made specifically for players who demand peak performance at all times.



◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




【Sound】

This instrument completely overturns the stereotype that ash bodies produce a cold, "scooped" (emphasized highs and lows) sound with a harsh, brittle edge. Instead, it delivers a superb, organic wood tone—distinctive of passive electronics—featuring a thick midrange that is pleasing to the ear.



It harnesses the crisp attack characteristic of ash while delivering a rich, "juicy," and substantial midrange—a hallmark of the MOON sound—that serves as the core of the musical ensemble.



When playing slap bass, the high frequencies are never piercing or painful; instead, the tone offers a pleasant, crisp snap that remains pleasantly mild and "juicy."



For fingerstyle playing, the organic, thick low-midrange—typical of passive basses—anchors the sound solidly, asserting the bassline's presence with a professional touch.

The pitch definition on the 5th string is clear, and the instrument feels complete right out of the box, relying solely on its natural resonance without the need for EQ adjustments.

It produces a supreme passive sound that doesn't cause ear fatigue during long sessions and drives the groove beautifully across a wide range of genres, from funk and R&B to vocal-driven pop.




【Playability】

The neck features a semi-gloss finish and a profile with just the right amount of thickness, offering a secure grip while prioritizing playability.

Many players find that high-gloss finishes can cause their fingers to stick or drag when sweating, but this finish avoids that issue. The semi-gloss finish—positioned between gloss and satin—combines the best qualities of both.


The fingerboard features a flat 400R (16-inch) radius; while the instrument boasts a vintage-inspired aesthetic, its specifications are thoroughly modern.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




【Specifications】

1. Wood Configuration

・Body: Ash

・Neck: Maple

・Fingerboard: Maple


2. Electronics

・Pickups: H.Y.G JB-5 (Moon original; delivers a wide-range sound with a solid core)

*Note: These were formerly branded as "OXALIS," but the brand name was changed as of January 1, 2026. There have been no changes to specifications such as wiring.

・Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone (Passive)


3. Hardware

・Tuners: GOTOH GBU510C-9

・Bridge: GOTOH 205B-5 (18mm spacing)

・Nut: Oil-impregnated bone (48mm width)

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




As this is a unique, one-off custom order, we have only a single unit in stock.

We invite you to make this instrument your new musical partner.



*Please note: While there appear to be scuffs on the pickguard, these marks are on the protective film applied at the factory, so the instrument itself is in pristine condition.



【Included Accessories】

Gig bag, wrench


【SPEC】

Body: Ash

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Maple

Frets: 21

Fretboard Radius: 400R (16")

Scale: 34" (864mm)

Pickups: H.Y.G JB-5

Bridge: GOTOH 205B-5 (18mm P.C.)

Tuning Machines: GOTOH GBU510C-9

item.title: Music Man StingRay Classic from 2010 in Coral Red — $3,642.17
item.description:
$3,642.17 · Good · No1 Guitar Center GmbH

This fabulous (used) Music Man stingRay classic is for sale in our store.

The Music Man StingRay Classic from 2010 pays tribute to one of the most influential electric bass designs in history, combining the unmistakable character of the original late-1970s StingRay with modern craftsmanship and premium build quality. Designed and manufactured in California, the StingRay Classic delivers the punch, clarity, and dynamic response that have made the model a favorite among professional bassists for decades.

At the heart of the StingRay Classic is Music Man’s iconic humbucking pickup paired with a powerful two-band active EQ. This combination produces the legendary StingRay tone, tight lows, articulate mids, and sparkling highs, making the bass equally at home in rock, funk, pop, and jazz settings. With its vintage-inspired appointments and exceptional versatility, the 2010 StingRay Classic faithfully captures the spirit and sound of the original while offering the reliability and craftsmanship for which Ernie Ball Music Man instruments are renowned.

The instrument will be shipped in the original hardcase.

Apart from a few possible minor changes, this guitar is original.

All instruments are in the shop and will be serviced before shipping.

For international safe & secure shipping: Please send your country, city and postal code.

We will calculate the rate for you door to door.

For the import in your country we cannot certify Lacey Act, TSCA or other regulations.

This has to be done by the buyer.

item.title: Music Man Sterling Bass from 1985 in Aged Buttercream — $2,925.67
item.description:
$2,925.67 · Good · No1 Guitar Center GmbH

This fabulous (used) Music Man Sterlin Bass from 1985 is for sale in our store.

The 1986 Music Man Sterling Bass represents one of the most important developments in the company's history. Introduced as a companion to the legendary StingRay, the Sterling offered a slimmer neck profile, a more compact body shape, and enhanced tonal flexibility, making it an instant favorite among demanding players.

Built in California to exceptionally high standards, the Sterling features a select hardwood body, a comfortable maple neck, and Music Man’s distinctive heavy-duty bridge design. Its powerful humbucking pickup, combined with an active three-band EQ, delivers a wide range of tones, from deep, punchy lows and articulate mids to crisp, sparkling highs. The result is a versatile instrument equally suited to rock, funk, pop, fusion, and studio work.

Renowned for its fast playability and dynamic response, the Sterling combines the unmistakable Music Man character with increased tonal control and a more refined feel. Today, early examples from 1986 are highly sought after by collectors and players alike, representing an important chapter in the evolution of one of the most respected names in electric bass design.

The instrument will be shipped in the original hardcase.

Apart from a few possible minor changes, this guitar is original.

All instruments are in the shop and will be serviced before shipping.

For international safe & secure shipping: Please send your country, city and postal code.

We will calculate the rate for you door to door.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1983 Fullerton Era '62 Reissue - Olympic White — $2,645.36
item.description:
$2,645.36 · Excellent · Fretboard Jungle

Here is a fine vintage 1983 Fender Jazz Bass - 1962 slab board reissue in aged Olympic White. Made in the USA as part of the renowned Fullerton era reissues.

By 1983, Fender were winding down their operations in Fullerton California. CBS sold Fender back to its employees in 1985, however the Fender factory in the original location of Fullerton California was sold separately. Early 80s "Dan Smith Era" instruments represent a return to vintage Fender quality and were made in the same factory as the originals, before manufacturing moved to Japan. 

This, like many of the early 80s Fender reissues features the CBS style logo rather than the vintage Fender "spaghetti" logo. It features a 62 style "Slab" board. The tan lines under the pick guard show significant yellowing over the years, giving this instrument a decidedly vintage look. 

This bass features original circuitry and hardware, with chrome stacked knobs, replacement 3 ply tortoiseshell pick guard and single coil pickups reading 6.9k ohms at the neck and 7.2k ohms at the bridge. 

The neck of this bass is slim and fast. 38.7mm nut width and a neck depth of 20mm at the first fret give this bass a very easy playing experience. The original reverse wound tuners have been replaced with later style forward winding tuners. One additional screw hole has been made to fasten each tuner.

Adjustability in the truss rod has been improved by adding a spacer under the truss rod nut. The replacement pickguard holes don't all line up perfectly, as such some holes have been relocated. There are also two "tug bar" holes on the body. The pickgard does not have these holes, so we know this is a replacement pickguard. 

Housed in its classic Fender tweed case, this vintage Fullerton made American Fender Jazz Bass marks an important era in the history of Fender and is both a collector piece and a serious contender for the working musician. 

______________________________

Fretboard Jungle is an independent guitar store specializing in vintage, rare and collectable guitars.

In-store pricing may vary. For more photos, videos and info find us on Instagram @fretboardjungle.

Also, please carefully read the shop policy located below the guitar description.


-----------------------------------------

Highly recommended for those who want the best of both the StingRay and Jazz Bass worlds!



【About this specific unit】

1. Appearance

The finish is a glossy black—the classic mirror-like finish.

It exudes a premium feel and shines beautifully under stage lighting.

It’s a popular, highly versatile color that fits any outfit or band concept; owning one ensures you're ready for any situation.

Paired with a tortoiseshell pickguard, it radiates a sophisticated, mature, vintage vibe.

The biggest advantage of a black bass is that you’ll never get tired of the look!


2. Weight: 4.38kg

For funk bassists who rely heavily on slapping, the instrument's density is crucial.

The solid alder body ensures string vibrations are fully utilized, producing a satisfying resonance: a solid, punchy low-end "thump" when striking with the thumb, and a brilliant, cutting "snap" when plucking with the index finger.


3. Setup

We’ve intentionally set the string height to a standard, comfortable range—approximately 2mm on the 1st string side and 2.5mm on the 4th string side. This allows for greater string amplitude, enhancing both the attack and the depth of the low end.



Although the serial number indicates a 2024 model, it arrived in our inventory in July 2026.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆



【Sound】

Thanks to the alder body, the sound avoids the harsh, glassy highs or stiffness often found in ash bodies; instead, it delivers a rich, warm low-midrange with a thick, solid presence.

True to its name, it produces a "Mighty" sound—a model that leans heavily into modern characteristics, prioritizing thickness and stability across all strings, from low to high.




The large humbucker pickup in the bridge position is the defining feature of this bass. The combination of the alder body's punchy mid-low range and the humbucker's massive output delivers superb sound quality—ranging from earth-shaking thumbing during slap playing to the loud, aggressive rock tones achieved by digging in with a pick.

Even on the 5-string model, this power ensures the sound projects forward with tremendous sonic impact.

A switch allows for instant coil-tapping of the bridge pickup.

This provides not only the humbucker's explosive power but also the sharp, crisp, and edgy tone characteristic of single-coil pickups.

It also allows for flexible, gig-ready dynamic control, such as instantly lowering the output to match the flow of the ensemble.




The tone control design reflects a meticulous attention to detail.

By intentionally limiting the sweep range compared to standard basses, the design prevents the sound from falling apart due to extreme tonal shifts, ensuring a "solid, usable tone" at any setting.

Even if your hand accidentally bumps the knob during a live performance, the sound won't become muddy or buried; it maintains a top-tier presence at all times.




In terms of genres, it pairs particularly well with funk, R&B, rock, punk, and pop.

Slap playing—leveraging the crisp highs and thick lows—is a hallmark of the StingRay style.

When played fingerstyle, every note is distinct, making it ideal for funky phrases that emphasize the groove.

It also delivers a gritty attack when played with a pick and retains a strong, solid core that doesn't get muddy even with distortion.

It powerfully supports the low end without getting lost in the mix, even amidst aggressive guitar sounds.

It is the perfect choice for players who want to provide solid support for vocal-driven songs while also being able to produce flashy, dynamic tones during slap sections or heavier parts.




【Playability】

It features the offset body design characteristic of Jazz Basses for excellent balance and includes a contoured neck heel for easy upper-fret access. Refined around the autumn of 2021, this model retains excellent playability in the high-fret range—allowing fingers to easily access the final frets—while increasing the contact area between the neck and body. This enhancement improves vibration transfer, resulting in a tighter, more focused sound.


The neck features a "U-shape" profile that strikes an exquisite balance—neither too thick nor too thin.

Renowned for being comfortable to grip without requiring excessive thumb pressure, it fits players of all hand sizes and is designed to minimize fatigue during long playing sessions.

It features a satin finish.

This neck finish is highly recommended for players who enjoy fast playing or those who wish to execute melodic bass lines.



◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆





【Specifications】

1. Wood Configuration

Alder body, maple neck, rosewood fretboard


2. Fujigen's Proprietary Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.)

This patented technology is essential to understanding this model.

On standard guitars, effective scale lengths vary slightly by string, leading to pitch discrepancies.

By addressing this issue and altering the fret installation method, pitch accuracy is significantly improved.


3. Electronics

Standard 2-volume, 1-tone configuration (with coil-tap function)

Pickups: Seymour Duncan Quarter Pound SJB-3n and SMB-4D


4. Bridge

Supports both string-through-body and bridge-end stringing methods.

String spacing is fixed at 19mm for both 4-string and 5-string models.


5. Scale Length: 34 inches

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




*The line visible on the back of the neck near the joint is caused by light reflection. [Included Accessories]

Soft case, hex key

Pickups: Seymour Duncan Quarter-Pound SJ4-3n, SMB-4D

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone with Push-Pull Switch (Coil Tap)

item.title: Ibanez EHB1675MS -Natural Flat-【USED】[GSB019] — $1,479.93
item.description:
$1,479.93 · Very Good · G-CLUB Produced by Kurosawa Music

Here is your chance if you missed out on buying this before.

It shows almost no signs of use and is in excellent condition.


A limited-production model designed to showcase the natural qualities of the wood.

This model delivers a superb finish that fully embodies the signature Ibanez spirit.


Ergonomic Headless Bass

A bass that combines the portability—the greatest advantage of the headless style—with exceptional playability. Above all, it reflects a thoughtful approach to bass tone. Ibanez Bass Workshop re-examined the headless concept—often criticized in the past for lacking resonance or being difficult to use—and created the EHB, a completely new kind of headless bass. It is neither an imitation of other headless models nor simply a headless version of an existing design or shape. This marks the birth of the EHB, a new breed of Ibanez bass.

The EHB body design is ergonomically crafted to ensure perfect balance against the player's body, whether standing or sitting.


The horn is shaped to balance the body and neck while standing and to make the instrument easy to hold, while the body curve on the treble-string side is designed specifically to optimize fit and balance when seated. The area where the forearm rests features a contoured slope. Additionally, all body edges that come into contact with the player's body are smoothly rounded. The back of the body is boldly cut to taper toward the bass strings; this design enhances the fit when seated and takes into account the viewing angle of the fretboard inlays. Furthermore, the back of the treble-side horn is scooped out to facilitate easy access to the upper frets.


Chambered body construction for a more tactile sense of resonance

By allowing the player to physically feel the internal vibrations—much like a hollow-body instrument—the design enables a better perception of the sound being produced. It also results in a slight reduction in overall weight.


Fishman® 3-band EQ with Single Coil Mode switch (push/pull) and 3-way Voice Select switch

The instrument utilizes a simple Fishman® 3-band equalizer. The center frequency for the Mid control is set at 630Hz. A push/pull function on the volume control allows you to select the mode for the two pickups; in Single Coil mode, only the inner coils of both the neck and bridge pickups are active.

Additionally, you can switch between three distinct voicings: Voice 1 (a classic, fat, and rounded sound) at the neck position, Voice 2 (a powerful, full-dynamic sound) at the bridge position, and Voice 2 Mid Contour (a funky, hi-fi sound with scooped mids) in the center position.

Combining the Single Coil mode with the voice selection offers a total of six tonal variations, and further blending with the balancer control allows for a wide range of sound shaping.


Fishman® Fluence™ pickups

These pickups are characterized by their wide dynamic range and multi-voicing capabilities, offering three distinct sound options.


SPEC

Neck type: EHB5 9pc Panga Panga/Walnut neck w/Graphite reinforcement rods

Body: Pale Moon Ebony top / Ash body

Fretboard: Bound Panga Panga fretboard w/Acrylic special inlay

Fret: Medium Stainless Steel

Bridge: MR5HS bridge (18mm string spacing)

Neck pickup: Fishman Fluence neck pickup

Bridge pickup: Fishman Fluence bridge pickup

Equalizer: Fishman 3-band EQ w/Single Coil Mode switch (push/pull), 3-way Voice Select switch

Machine Head: Ibanez custom headpieces

Hardware color: Black


Weight: Approx. 3.8kg

Original Gig Case included

Consignment item

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!
This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar from Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reuse/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------
A custom color version of the Active JB flagship model, "M#265"!

The M (Magunitude) #265 was originally released as a signature model for Tomohito Aoki, but it is now used by countless musicians, both professional and amateur, and is a standard model and a representative model of Atelier Z. Based on the 70s JB tone, it has clear highs and a thick low end, and the sound produced from the tightly grained ash body is the epitome of the classic scooped bass sound, and its sound has a unique and unparalleled character.

This model features the Spectrum Boost that Mr. Aoki had been seeking, resulting in a significantly increased sense of power compared to previous models of the same type. In addition to the bold sound he is known for when slapping, it offers a new sonic potential that can handle a wide range of genres. Furthermore, this model features a custom transparent black color, giving it a cool look with the wood grain showing through. By deliberately using a non-matching specification, the blackness of the body stands out, making it a truly exceptional instrument. Please try this instrument, which exudes a different kind of charm from the regular M#265.

The control has been changed from the conventional [2 Volume] to a [Master Volume, Balancer] configuration for smoother control, and the controls are now "Master Volume, Balancer, /Master Tone (Pull/Passive) Treble/Bass".

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!

The M (Magunitude) #245 was released as a signature model for Tomohito Aoki, but it is now used by countless musicians, both professional and amateur, and is a standard model and a representative model of Atelier Z.

Based on the JB tone of the 70s, it boasts clear highs and a powerful low end, and the sound produced by the densely grained ash body delivers the quintessential scooped bass sound. The control system consists only of two volume controls and a 2-band EQ boost for treble and bass, but opening the back panel reveals a Mid-CUT trim, allowing you to adjust it to your preferred settings.
Attention to detail is evident in features such as the neck angle, making it easy to produce sound even with a light touch. It can handle a variety of playing styles, from the bold sound of his signature slap technique to the thick tone of fingerstyle playing and the attack of pick playing, making it a versatile instrument that can be used in any genre.
Furthermore, this model incorporates the Spectrum Boost that Mr. Aoki had been seeking, resulting in a significantly increased sense of power compared to previous models of the same type. In addition to the bold sound he is known for when slapping, it offers a new sonic potential that can handle a wide range of genres.

The body top features a black base with gold lines following the wood grain, creating a dazzling stage presence with varying degrees of shine depending on the angle of the light. The headstock features a matte black finish, giving it a refined look. Enjoy this custom model from our store.

Body: Ash 2 Pieces
Neck: Maple 1 Piece 34inch
Fingerboard: Maple, 20 Frets
Pickups : Atelier z Magnitude JBZ-4
Bridge: Atelier z BB419
Preamp: Bartorini XTCT
Controls: M.Vol, Balancer, M.Tone/sw, Treble/Bass

The popular ukulele bass, KALA U-BASS.

KALA is an American ukulele manufacturer founded in Petaluma, California in 2005. They offer high-quality ukuleles at reasonable prices, with a lineup of nearly 150 models including travel models, ukulele basses, and a wide variety of woods. They boast the No. 1 ukulele sales share in the continental United States and enjoy immense popularity in Hawaii, the birthplace of the ukulele. The U-BASS, first introduced in 2008, is a popular model that uses specially designed strings that are thicker and more elastic than regular bass strings made of a rubber-like material.

The Scout U-BASS is a more decorative version of the Nomad, positioned a step above the other affordable models in the U-BASS series. Featuring cream binding and a Venetian cutaway, it's recommended for bassists who want to play in higher positions. It also boasts the excellent picking response and well-defined sound characteristic of mahogany, resulting in a U-BASS with rich volume.

There are some minor scratches and light dents, but it is in good working order and can be used without any problems.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck is in good condition.

The M#245, a super standard model from our atelier, is now available as a custom-ordered model from our store!
While this brand's basses often feature transparent colors that showcase the ash wood grain, this particular instrument deliberately opts for a solid Lake Placid Blue finish, combined with gold hardware to create a sharp, rock-and-roll, and cool look.

ATELIER Z enjoys immense popularity among bassists, both professional and amateur, especially those who are dedicated to slap bass playing. The M (Magunitude) #245 was originally released as a signature model for Tomohito Aoki, but it is now used by countless musicians, both professional and amateur, and is considered a standard model and a flagship model of ATELIER Z.

The combination of an ash body and maple fingerboard delivers a clear, punchy tone with tight bass. The control system includes one volume, pickup balancer, master tone, and a 2-band EQ with treble/bass boost. Opening the back panel reveals a Mid-CUT trim, allowing for a wide range of adjustments to your preferred settings. The treble/bass controls have a click stop at 50% to indicate the preamp boost level, making it easy to adjust to your liking. This unit features a wiring configuration called Spectrum Boost, which increases sound pressure and allows for an even wider range of sound shaping.

This model boasts reliable quality and materials that can keep pace with the wide range of high-end amplifiers.
With its excellent responsiveness, allowing the sound to project smoothly even when played effortlessly, and its combination of clarity and a wild side, this guitar can handle a variety of playing styles, from thick fingerstyle tones and explosive slap sounds to the attack of pick playing, making it suitable for any genre. If you're looking for a ready-to-use active model, this is it!

"Easy-Wheel" Dual Action Truss Rod and 2 pcs of Double Titanium Reinforcement Rods. All Woodcraft basses utilize Dual Truss Rods “Easy Wheel” truss rod adjustment system made from Titanium to maintain neck stiffness, stability and reduce weight. Easy Wheel allows you to make the adjustment on the spot without special wrenches.

There are a lot of basses on the market with a tendency to have weak neck binding structures due to high string tensions. For this reason, two Titanium rods are applied to all bass guitars.

TUSQ NUT: The nut is one of the most important parts of the bass that influences the performance, playability, and tone of the instrument. Depending on the quality of the material, the nut can either improve or degrade the sound. This rock-solid workhorse instrument from G&L's affordable Tribute Series is all about refinement — taking the iconic J-bass formula and refining it by blending "holy grail" Alnico single-coil tone with modern Leo Fender-designed innovations.

For starters, the JB-2 has a more contemporary "vibe" with its comfortable body shape inspired by G&L's L Series models. The slim Maple neck features a smooth satin finish, while the relatively flat 12" radius and medium-jumbo nickel frets deliver modern playability. What's more, the JB-2 boasts G&L 's acclaimed 'Saddle-Lock' bridge that transfers string energy right into the body for incredible resonance!

item.title: Epiphone Thunderbird-IV Electric Bass — $519.27
item.description:
$519.27 · Good · Guitar Buyer

Thank you so much for viewing our listings! ☆


----------------------------------------------------------------------


【PRODUCT DESCRIPTION】


■Epiphone Thunderbird-IV Electric Bass


■Features

The Epiphone Thunderbird-IV is an electric bass that makes an unforgettable impression with its distinctive reverse body shape. Inheriting the legacy of the Gibson Thunderbird, this instrument carries a commanding presence and immediately evokes a rock atmosphere the moment you pick it up.


The sunburst-finished body beautifully showcases wood grain detail in the center with deep coloring around the edges, achieving a harmonious balance between classic aesthetics and striking visual appeal. The combination with black hardware is excellent, and this bass looks fantastic on stage.


Equipped with dual pickups and a straightforward, user-friendly design, this instrument delivers a thick, commanding bass tone. Highly recommended for players seeking a character that cuts through in rock, punk, and hard rock band settings.


The Thunderbird's distinctive body balance combined with the tight, responsive feel characteristic of a long-scale bass ensures this model is enjoyable both visually and in terms of playability. Those seeking an individual character distinct from standard Jazz Bass or Precision Bass models will find this particularly appealing.


Black-finished headstock, tuning pegs, bridge, and knobs give the instrument a cohesive look, creating striking contrast with the sunburst body. Neither overly flashy nor subdued, this bass strikes an impressive balance and delivers high satisfaction whether used for live performance or as a collectible piece.


While showing scattered scratches, wear marks, dings, minor paint cracks on the neck, and missing pickguard sections, this used bass remains in playable condition with plenty of life left in it. If you're searching for a practical, unassuming instrument to play freely without worry about cosmetic wear, this is an excellent choice.


The Thunderbird-IV remains popular in the used market, combining distinctive styling with approachable playability—exactly the kind of excellent value Epiphone is known for. If you're interested in enjoying authentic rock bass character at an accessible price point, we encourage you to consider this opportunity.

item.title: GUYATONE SB-25 - c.1968 ***RARE*** SEMI-ACOUSTIC BASS w/OHSC — $1,849.91
item.description:
$1,849.91 · Excellent · VEJ Guitars

Guyatone SB-25 ***Rare & Vintage*** 1960s Semi-acoustic Bass w/OHSC


A very rare Japanese semi-acoustic bass from the late 1960s - especially in this condition.

Even comes with its original hard case.

All-original and gorgeous!


The companion model guitars, Guyatone's SG-25 come along sometimes, but this bass version is much rarer.

If the shape looks familiar it's because the body and parts were made in the same plant as the Yamaha SA-70. The body is identical but with a modified heel for this bolt-on neck.

Like the SA-70, the build quality and finishing is faultless.


Condition-wise...

Exceptional. Hard to believe this is from the late 1960s.

Barely a blemish worth photographing.

The body and bindings are all in fabulous shape.

The short-scale neck (30") is very slim and comfortable. The rosewood fretboard is also very good and the frets are in great shape - plenty of life left.

All the hardware, controls, bridge, pickups etc are original. Original closed chrome tuners work perfectly. Electrics are all smooth and controls are also original.

Original wooden trapeze tailpiece and heavy chrome fully adjustable bridge.



As a glance at our other listings will show, we are BIG Guyatone fans. Their pickups especially.

The pickups here are the bass versions of the superb P-90 style pickups you can see on other Guyatone models. They have fantastic tone quality and are very responsive.


This is an absolute delight to play.

Action is comfortable and smooth, intonation is great and the vintage tones it puts out are quite sublime.



In Summary...

A rare vintage 60s semi-acoustic MIJ bass in excellent condition with the original hard case included.

Very high quality build - it plays just as great as it looks.


This is a rare & collectable find.

Keeper!

  • Double bound hollow body in original black finish
  • Slim Maple neck - 30" Scale
  • Bound rosewood fretboard & headstock - 21 frets (incl 'Zero' Fret) w/dot position markers

item.title: EDWARDS E-J-85MF White LUNA SEA J Signature 【3.91㎏】 — $619.14
item.description:
$619.14 · Very Good · Yamanashi Musical Instrument Store

The EDWARDS E-J-85MF White is the signature bass of J, bassist of the legendary Japanese rock band LUNA SEA. Combining the iconic SG Bass silhouette with modern playability, this model is designed for powerful stage performance.

Based on the classic SG Bass shape, the instrument features a uniquely contoured back that improves comfort and provides an excellent fit against the player's body, making it comfortable even during long performances.

Equipped with 24 frets, the bass offers easy access to the upper register, making it well suited for technical playing and expressive live performances.

The dual-pickup configuration, combined with independent two-volume and two-tone controls, provides a wide range of tones, from thick rock sounds to articulate modern bass tones.

Weighing just 3.91kg, the bass remains lightweight and well balanced while retaining the distinctive feel of an SG-style instrument.

Its White finish gives the bass a striking stage appearance that perfectly complements J's signature style.

Cosmetically, the bass shows a few minor scratches and light marks from normal use, but overall remains in very good condition.

You know what it is. The active jazz bass based on the custom shop version used live. It is Fender’s take on a Modulus bass, and is awesome. Colour is closer to online photos, it photographed weird. The most solid feeling fender I have ever played, decently big, comfy neck that feels great. Both neck and body are satin. Aguilar 2 band preamp, with a boost on the last bit of the volume knob that gives a pretty hefty boost. I normally played with it on 9, as it felt similar to an 18V active bass volume there. No wear or damage, really mint. Comes with hardshell case and case candy. I can’t find the original strap buttons, but they were just stock silver fender ones if you needed new ones. Will include a strap with locks, and a few pairs of pins you can use on your straps if you decide to keep them on.

item.title: 2025 Fender Japan Limited Collection Traditional 60s Jazz Bass – Black Pearl — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Excellent · Made in Japan Bass Collection

For Sale: 2025 Fender Japan Limited Collection Traditional 60s Jazz Bass – Black Pearl

Up for sale is a rare 2025 Fender Made in Japan Limited Collection Traditional 60s Jazz Bass in a striking Black Pearl finish. This instrument is in Excellent condition with moderate cosmetic blemishes and comes complete with the original case candy and Fender gig bag. (Please note: The hardshell case pictured is for display purposes only and is not included).

The Instrument

Part of a highly limited, special factory run for the 2025 Collection, the Jazz Bass is one of the rarest models in this series. The Black Pearl finish is a stunning metallic black paired elegantly with premium gold hardware.

Built on the foundation of the acclaimed Traditional 60s platform, the successor to the beloved '62 Reissue (JB62), this model brings vintage spec into the modern era while retaining the soul, quirks, and unmistakable mojo of the original MIJ classics.

Craftsmanship & Playability

Crafted at the legendary Dyna Gakki factory, this bass represents the pinnacle of their post-2022 retooling and modernization. The build quality coming out of this factory easily rivals, and often eclipses, its American counterparts.

True to its JB62 heritage, the body and neck profile are slightly slimmer than standard vintage-spec Jazz Basses, making it an incredibly fast, comfortable, and ergonomic player. The highly resonant basswood body is paired with a beautiful dark rosewood slab board. Electronically, the vintage-style single-coils deliver a wonderful, warm classic thump, but with a refined modern punch that cuts through any mix.

Condition & Setup

  • Setup: Professionally dialed-in and ready to play straight out of the box. Strung with D'Addario NYXL (45–100). Action is set low and fast at 2.0mm on the E string and 1.75mm on the G string.
  • Cosmetics: Rated Excellent. The bass has two finish blemishes (moderate dings/dents) located on the bottom edge and the arm contour. Both are strictly cosmetic and do not affect playability. (Please refer to pictures 13 and 14 for close-ups).

Specifications:

  • Body Material: Basswood
  • Body Finish: Gloss Polyester (Black Pearl Metallic)
  • Neck Profile: Fender Japan Slim Taper "C"
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood (Slab Board)
  • Fingerboard Radius: 9.5"
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Frets: 20 Vintage
  • Pickups: Fender Japan Vintage-Style Single-Coil Jazz Bass (Neck & Bridge)
  • Hardware: Gold
  • Weight: 8.5 lbs (Lightweight and perfectly balanced)
  • Inclusions: Original Fender factory gig bag and case candy

If you have any questions or would like additional photos, please feel free to send us a message!

item.title: Ibanez SRMD200D Mezzo Medium Scale 32" bass with rare Jatoba Fretboard! Look! — $259.99
item.description:
$259.99 · Very Good · Lenny's Bass Guitars & Stuff

This is my Ibanez SRMD200D "Mezzo" bass. The term "Mezzo" is an Italian word meaning "Middle". This bass is "Middle" scale, or "Medium" scale, at 32". It is painted Pearl White and has the ever-elusive Jatoba fretboard !  I have owned this bass for about 1 year and it has been the recipient of different pickups. When all is said-and-done, I prefer this stock setup over all the other pickups I've tried. The stock pickups, coupled with the active Bass and Treble boost/cut work wonderfully together.

In the course of changing pickups, I managed to chip the finish in some small areas: The paint by the back control cavity cover was chipped trying to remove it. I glued the chip back in, but it's still obvious. On the back, though. After doing that, I fabricated a small groove in both that cover and the battery cover, for ease of removal. I wish Ibanez would have done that initially! The rout for the Jazz (bridge side) pickup got chipped slightly, too. I applied a very small amount of off-white paint to hide/protect it.  See pics. The only way I notice them is when I'm taking pics! Normally, the shadow cast from the jazz pickup is enough to where it isn't noticeable. So, aside from those little issues, this is a super nice and beautiful bass, in fit, form and function.

I have owned a few of these SRMD200's and they are a blast to play. However, I have 2 other basses with medium scale and this one doesn't get played much. I'm sure someone will enjoy it a lot. 

These Ibanez Mezzo SRMD200D's were introduced in 2021, then dropped from Ibanez' lineup in 2023. Now it's getting harder to find a used Mezzo bass, much less the Mezzo with the Jatoba fretboard. Only 2 colors were offered with this fretboard: Forest Green and Pearl White. The SRMD200 Mezzos were introduced in 2019, with maple fretboards were offered in 5 or 6 colors. I still own my Mezzo 5 string and will never get rid of that one! Meanwhile, here is this fine specimen of an Ibanez SRMD200D bass offered to you folks. 

Please have a look at all the pics as they are also a part of the description.

I will ship as soon as payment clears, insured and signature required, via the carrier of my choice. 

Feel free to contact me with questions/comments.

Thanks for looking! - Lenny

item.title: Vintage Fender 2000 American Standard Jazz Bass, RW FB, 3-Color Sunburst W/TSA Case — $1,195
item.description:
$1,195 · Very Good · Haight Ashbury Music Center

Vintage 2000 American Standard Jazz Bass, Rosewood Fingerboard, 3-Color Sunburst, W/TSA Hardshell Case

Dialed in and set up in-shop; plays great with low action and no issues. Very clean overall, some finish wear, some small dings on hip, buckle rash on back. Spot on back of neck where finish is worn, some wear on headstock. Includes original hardshell case. Case is fully functional, some light cosmetic wear and small crack on corner.

Description:

The Fender American Standard Jazz Bass combines the legendary tone and feel of the world's most popular offset bass with modern refinements for today's professional player. Featuring a select alder body, a comfortable modern C-shaped maple neck, a rosewood fingerboard with 20 medium jumbo frets, and two American Standard Jazz Bass single-coil pickups, it delivers the unmistakable punch, growl, and articulation that have made the Jazz Bass an industry standard.

Additional features include a deluxe string-through-body bridge for increased sustain and resonance, sealed Fender/Schaller tuning machines for precise tuning stability, chrome hardware, and Fender's classic volume/volume/master tone control layout. Built in Corona, California, the American Standard Jazz Bass offers exceptional craftsmanship, dependable performance, and timeless Fender style.

Nice example of Aria's very high quality precision basses from the 1970s.

These are seriously well-made.

Rod has plenty of adjustment available.

Finish has worn off the control knobs, but again, it's purely cosmetic.

Strong output, classic precision depth and super-punch.

Good flat wound strings.


Overall...

A good clean example of an all-original, high-quality Matsumoku-built precision in nice condition.

Years of service to come.

Plays well, intonation is tight and it really pumps it out.


Solid as it gets.

item.title: Fender Standard Performer Bass 1985 - 1987 - Frost White — $1,800
item.description:
$1,800 · Good · Wishingchair Guitars

~1985 Fender Performer Bass Guitar

This is a pretty rare/cool made in Japan Fender bass, only made from 1985-1987. It's gotta a pencil thin neck and would be good for someone with small hands. It's a rocker, plays well, sounds like a Fender bass to me, but has a little tonal versatility. Here is the wikipedia:


  • All original.
  • Good condition with fairly many dings and scuffs, see pictures
  • Includes non-original but well fitting hard shell case as shown.

It does have a few minor issues:

  • As pictured, there is a chip in the fretboard above the nut, as well as a small divot on the first fret, along with what looks like a little burn on the head stock (maybe from a soldering iron, I dunno. None of these cause any practical/play issues, but it might be worth having a luthier cut a small piece of rosewood and glue it in there so the nut is fully supported. I did re-glue the nut recently.
  • There is a bit of string buzz at the lowest frets, coudl be adjusted a little. Was not a big deal to me in terms of playing and though.
  • The jack was a little bit touchy, I used some contact cleaner. It might be worth replacing, but again, working as-is.
  • The tone knob ("TBX" tone knob or whatnot, the lowest knob towards the bottom) seems to spin past its upper end point. Working fine, the center detente is fine, no sound issues, but might need to be a little gentle with it. It could be the knob though.
  • The aforementioned dings and scratches.

Please let me know if you have any questions and thanks for looking--

A note on shipping/handling: it really depends on your location. If it ends up costing much less I will refund the difference.

item.title: Ibanez EDA900- Ergodyne Bass 1990 - Silver Flat — $625
item.description:
$625 · Very Good · Iguana MUSIC

The EDA900 is an Ergodyne series solid body electric bass model introduced by Ibanez for 2001. It was produced in South Korea by Cor-Tek.

The EDA900 features a luthite body bolted to a maple neck with a 24-fret rosewood fingerboard with offset abalone dot position markers at the twelfth fret and white side dots. The body has a sculpted, asymmetrical double cutaway design with distinctive, narrow, curving horns; the logo is inlaid into the guitar top between the pickup and bridge. Components include a single "Ibanez Dynamix Full Range" DFR-N5 pickup mounted halfway between the bridge, an EQA-PZ system EQ with piezo active tone control, a Mono-rail II bridge with individual volume adjustable polyphonic piezo saddle pickups and 16.5mm string spacing, a barrel-style stereo output jack, a plastic nut and die-cast tuning machines.

item.title: RICKENBACKER - BASSE STEREO 4003 W/C MG - GRI 4003MG — $3,103.81
item.description:
$3,103.81 · Brand New · Hurricane Music

Le grain Rickenbacker, en stéréo

La Rickenbacker 4003 est une basse iconique au caractère immédiatement reconnaissable : attaque franche, médiums présents et définition parfaite pour percer dans un mix. Cette version Mapleglo met en valeur l’érable et s’adresse aux bassistes qui veulent une signature sonore vintage avec la fiabilité d’un instrument moderne.

Grâce à sa sortie stéréo, vous pouvez séparer les micros pour traiter chaque source différemment (deux amplis, deux chaînes d’effets, enregistrement multipiste). La construction manche traversant apporte sustain et stabilité, et l’étui rigide inclus sécurise les déplacements.

Caractéristiques
  • Corps : érable
  • Manche : érable, traversant avec skunk stripe
  • Touche : palissandre des Caraïbes, non verni
  • Repères : triangulaires
  • Electronique : sortie stéréo
  • Barres de réglage : double truss rod 2 voies
  • Nombre de Frettes : 20
  • Diapason : 845 mm
  • Largeur du Sillet : 42,9 mm
  • Micro Manche : simple bobinage basse
  • Micro Chevalet : simple bobinage basse avec capot ouvert et repose-pouce
  • Mécaniques : Schaller Deluxe

item.title: Vintage Precision Bass Luthier Project — $199
item.description:
$199 · Non Functioning · Guitar Buyer

Sold as is, untested, as pictured. Selling this vintage P-Bass for a repair project. This bass is very heavy. The fretboard has a hairline crack, from the nut to the 2nd fret.

Missing the pickguard assembly and a tuning key. The body top is damaged where the end of the pickguard mounts. Circled on the 2nd photo.

I will include a tuning key for the one that is missing.

I will be removing the neck from the body and padding each piece separately in a box. If you do not have someone who can put it back together, please refrain from bidding.

item.title: Ken Smith HF5 E 2026 - Frost Gold — $4,100
item.description:
$4,100 · Brand New · Bass Japan Direct

This MIJ Hadrien Feraud Burner sports two very cool new colors in this line up for 2026.

This model has Hadrien's preferred preamp and control set, with the added mid control to get that warm, fat creamy burp on the rear pickup. It produces rich mids and focused lows, while the highs are nice and clear but solid.

Wonderful character, ideal for jazz, funk and fusion players.

The action is super smooth and low with very accurate intonation and great sustain.

Body. Solid ash

Neck. 3 pc maple

Fretboard. Ebony

Frets. 24 stainless steel

Grip. Thin D

Tuners. OEM by Gotoh

Bridge. OEM Ken Smith original

Preamp. Original 3 band

Controls. Vol/bypass, bal, treble, bass, mid

Weight. 4.3kg average

Case. High grade semi-hard case by Music Area
